sourceMovie.title: Attack on Titan II: End of the World
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Attack on Titan II: End of the World works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Gantz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Attack on Titan II: End of the World clicked for you, Gantz (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Cloverfield

faq.question: What should I watch after Attack on Titan II: End of the World?
faq.answer: Try Gantz, Parasyte: Part 1, Cloverfield—each shares DNA with Attack on Titan II: End of the World while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Attack on Titan II: End of the World?
faq.answer: Attack on Titan II: End of the World (2015) is best described as Horror, Science Fiction, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Attack on Titan II: End of the World part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Attack on Titan (Live-Action) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
